metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC #1, replace 'per the removed-key backcompat test' with a concrete proving command (e.g. `pytest tests/test_config_schema.py::test_removed_key_warned_and_ignored -v`) or, if no such test yet exists, add a sub-bullet instructing the executor to write one that asserts a `[reconciler]` section containing `baseline_dual_write` or `baseline_consumer_swap` emits a warning and does not raise.
findings.finding: AC #1 references 'per the removed-key backcompat test' as the proving mechanism for the warn-and-ignore behavior when either removed flag appears in a config, but does not identify where that test lives, what it tests, or whether it already exists. If no such test or framework exists, the executor has no proving command and must invent the infrastructure from scratch without guidance, leaving the AC unverifiable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #1 ('each is now an unknown key … per the removed-key backcompat test')

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the error-handling posture of the extracted `_advance_baselines` helper: state explicitly whether it retains, drops, or narrows the current `try/except BLE001` wrapper from `reconcile.py:950`, and justify the choice (e.g., 'set_baseline is pure in-memory and cannot raise I/O errors, so no wrapper is needed' OR 'retain fail-open to preserve the no-break-sync invariant for the persist phase').
findings.finding: The plan promotes the baseline advancement call from a fail-open, optional shadow invocation to an unconditional always-on operation, but does not specify the error-handling posture of the extracted `_advance_baselines` helper — specifically, whether it retains the current broad `try/except BLE001` wrapper (whose explicit rationale, 'shadow is derisk-only; never break sync', no longer applies once it is production behavior) or is promoted to fail-closed / a narrower guard. This is a missing positive rationale for a real design decision: fail-open keeps the current behavior (a transient error silently skips advancement for that pass, baselines fall behind by one pass) while fail-closed would abort the sync pass on any advancement error.
findings.location: What section / 'First EXTRACT the set_baseline advancement loop into a standalone helper (e.g. reconcile._advance_baselines) called UNCONDITIONALLY before save()'; reconcile.py:935-954 (current fail-open wrapper); baseline_shadow.py:84 (set_baseline call)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'What' section: 'The extracted `_advance_baselines` helper runs without a broad exception wrapper (the set_baseline loop is pure in-memory and cannot fail on I/O); if a future implementation adds fallible I/O, a targeted guard should be added at that time.' Alternatively, retain the existing wrapper with an updated comment that replaces 'shadow is derisk-only' with 'baseline advancement must not abort the sync pass if a store entry is unexpectedly missing or malformed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the 'What' section exactly which state/counters `_advance_baselines` carries (e.g., does it return a count of cold-started bindings? does it emit the cold-start line internally?), distinguishing these from the equivalence-check counters (`equal`, `divergent`) that are deleted.
findings.finding: The `_advance_baselines` extraction is structurally ambiguous: the current `run_dual_write_shadow` interleaves `seeded/equal/divergent` counter increments with `set_baseline` calls in a single loop. The plan says to 'EXTRACT the set_baseline advancement loop into a standalone helper' and 'delete ONLY the equivalence-check portions', but does not specify whether `_advance_baselines` retains any counter state (e.g., a `seeded` count for the cold-start log line) or whether the cold-start log emission is inside or outside this helper. This is an ambiguous counter/state increment placement.
findings.location: 'What' section, paragraph beginning 'First EXTRACT the set_baseline advancement loop'; baseline_shadow.py:57-84

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the 'What' section whether `_advance_baselines` is called inside a fail-open `try/except` (recommended, to preserve the existing 'never break sync' contract) or bare, and if bare, why that is safe.
findings.finding: The existing `run_dual_write_shadow` call in `reconcile.py:941-954` is wrapped in a `try/except BLE001` fail-open guard ('shadow is derisk-only; never break sync'). The plan instructs calling `_advance_baselines` 'UNCONDITIONALLY before save()' but does not specify whether the fail-open wrapper is retained. If dropped, a `set_baseline` failure (e.g., a corrupt `bindings.json` partially written) will abort the sync pass — a regression. This is a decision rule not co-located with the step that needs it.
findings.location: 'What' section ('called UNCONDITIONALLY before save()'); reconcile.py:935-954
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Retain a `try/except Exception` (BLE001) wrapper around the `_advance_baselines()` call at `reconcile.py:~938`, matching the existing guard on `run_dual_write_shadow`, so a baseline write failure cannot abort a sync pass.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step to audit all prose docs (README, operator runbooks, config reference, CHANGELOG) for references to `baseline_dual_write` and `baseline_consumer_swap` and update or remove them.
findings.finding: The plan removes `baseline_dual_write` and `baseline_consumer_swap` from schema, parser, and rebar.toml, but does not call for an audit of other documentation artifacts (README, operator runbooks, config-reference docs, CHANGELOG) that may still reference these flags by name, leaving stale references.
findings.location: ## What — 'Remove both fields + validators from ReconcilerConfig' and AC item 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `grep -r 'baseline_dual_write\|baseline_consumer_swap' docs/` step to the implementation checklist, and update any hits to note the flags are retired and the behavior is now always-on.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly decide the fate of `seed_baselines_from_snapshot()` in the plan: either delete it (if it is truly superseded by lazy population) or add a prominent docstring/comment explaining it is a manual recovery tool for the cold-start window and is intentionally not called automatically.
findings.finding: `seed_baselines_from_snapshot()` is explicitly called out as existing but never called; after this change it will be a zombie function coexisting with the new `_advance_baselines` helper, with no plan to remove it, comment it, or document why it is kept — creating a maintainability trap for future contributors.
findings.location: ## Cold-start note — 'seed_baselines_from_snapshot() exists but is never called'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a docstring to `seed_baselines_from_snapshot()` such as: 'Manual recovery helper — call once via a migration script if a repo has never dual-written baselines. Not called automatically; lazy population via _advance_baselines() is the normal path. See docs/jira-sync-setup.md §Cold-start window.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_baseline_consumer_swap.py` to the edit plan: either delete it (and add a replacement test that exercises the unconditional always-on arbitration path) or refactor it to remove the `baseline_consumer_swap=False` branch tests and keep only the baseline-as-ancestor behavioral assertions.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_baseline_consumer_swap.py` is an unenumerated consumer: the entire file tests `OutboundDiffConfig(baseline_consumer_swap=...)` and `_diff_fields(..., baseline_consumer_swap=False/True)`. After the field is removed from `OutboundDiffConfig` and the boolean parameter is collapsed in `outbound_fields.py`, these tests will either raise `TypeError` (unexpected kwarg) or silently test nothing meaningful.
findings.location: What section (boolean-threading collapse) / tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_baseline_consumer_swap.py
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the file and add a focused replacement (e.g., `test_baseline_arbitration_always_on.py`) that exercises direction-suppression and both-sides-conflict unconditionally, verifying the same behavioral invariants without the flag toggle.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_baseline_shadow.py` to the edit plan: remove or update tests that assert on `baseline_shadow_check`/`baseline_shadow_divergence` events and the RECON stderr line; if `_advance_baselines` moves to `reconcile.py`, migrate the advancement-loop tests accordingly.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_baseline_shadow.py` is an unenumerated consumer: it tests `run_dual_write_shadow` — including the equivalence-check assertions (`baseline_shadow_check`, `baseline_shadow_divergence` event names, the `RECON:` stderr line) — all of which are in the portion the plan says to delete.
findings.location: What section ('delete ONLY the equivalence-check portions') / tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_baseline_shadow.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_file_wiring.py` (lines 203-218) to the edit plan: remove `test_baseline_consumer_swap_default_false` and `test_baseline_consumer_swap_rebar_toml_roundtrip`, and replace them with (or fold into `test_config_backcompat.py`) the removed-key warn-and-ignore tests required by AC #1.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_file_wiring.py` has two unenumerated tests (`test_baseline_consumer_swap_default_false` at line 204 and `test_baseline_consumer_swap_rebar_toml_roundtrip` at line 209) that assert on `cfg.load_config(...).reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap`. After `baseline_consumer_swap` is removed from `ReconcilerConfig`, both tests will raise `AttributeError`.
findings.location: What section (schema/parser removal) / tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_file_wiring.py:203-218
findings.suggested_fix: Delete both tests from `test_config_file_wiring.py` and add parameterized warn-and-ignore assertions for `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` and `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap` into `tests/unit/test_config_backcompat.py`, paralleling the existing `test_removed_verdict_alias_ignored_and_warns` pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit task in the 'What' section to create the removed-key backcompat tests: write tests (in `test_config_backcompat.py` or `test_config_file_wiring.py`) asserting that `[reconciler] baseline_dual_write = true` and `[reconciler] baseline_consumer_swap = true` each produce a warning-and-ignore (not an error) when loaded, mirroring the `test_removed_verdict_alias_ignored_and_warns` pattern.
findings.finding: AC #1 requires 'a removed-key backcompat test' asserting that each retired flag name is warned-and-ignored. No such test currently exists in the repo for these reconciler keys — `test_config_backcompat.py` only covers `verify.require_verdict_for_close`. The plan's 'What' section never tasks the implementer with creating this test, creating a gap between AC and the work decomposition.
findings.location: AC #1 / What section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope the `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` edits: delete or rewrite the 'Convergence rollout' section (lines 317-355), the 'Baseline dual-write shadow' subsection (lines 468-477), and the flag-based rollback instruction at line 483; replace with the always-on warm-up window documentation called for by AC #4.
findings.finding: `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` has a 'Rollback procedure (summary)' at line 483 that instructs operators to 'set `reconciler.baseline_dual_write = false`' as the step-2 rollback action after a baseline swap misbehaves. After both flags are removed and always-on is hardcoded, this instruction becomes stale and potentially dangerous (operators following it will get a 'unknown key' warning and no actual rollback). The plan says to update `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` but does not specify that this rollback instruction must be rewritten or removed.
findings.location: What section ('docs/jira-sync-setup.md') / docs/jira-sync-setup.md:479-487

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ordered first step to AC #6: before any code edits, run `reconcile --dry-run` on a fixed fixture with flags currently true and commit the captured stdout as `tests/fixtures/reconcile_dry_run_baseline.txt`; the new test then diffs the post-change output against this committed artifact.
findings.finding: AC #6 requires a byte-identical 'BEFORE' capture of `reconcile --dry-run` stdout from the pre-change codebase, but the plan lists no explicit ordered first step to capture and commit this fixture BEFORE any code edits begin. An executor who starts modifying code first cannot reconstruct the valid pre-change baseline, making the comparison fixture permanently invalid or fabricated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #6 (dry-run before/after diff)
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend to AC #6: 'Step 0 (do this first, before any code change): run `python -m reconciler reconcile --dry-run --store tests/fixtures/reconcile_fixture_store` and commit stdout as `tests/fixtures/reconcile_dry_run_baseline.txt`. The new regression test diffs post-change output against this committed artifact and asserts zero diff.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'What' bullet (or annotate the cold-start section) explicitly listing the two implementation steps: (1) emit `RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<id>` in the unconditional `get_baseline()` call path when the result is `None`, and (2) add a unit test asserting the log line fires on a cold-start binding.
findings.finding: The 'What' section (the implementer's guide) does not describe adding the `RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<id>` stderr log line or its accompanying unit test — those appear only in the 'Cold-start note' section and AC bullet 4. An implementer following 'What' alone would ship cold-start observability without the log line and without the test, leaving AC bullet 4 unverifiable.
findings.location: 'What' section (implementation steps) vs. 'Cold-start note' section and AC bullet 4
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add a bullet: 'In the unconditional `get_baseline()` read path (outbound_fields.py:418-419), emit `RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<id>` to stderr whenever the result is `None`; add a unit test that asserts this line fires for a confirmed binding with no stored baseline.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the 'What' section a bullet describing the dry-run regression test: create a fixed reconcile fixture store, capture `--dry-run` stdout before and after the flag removal, assert byte-identical output, and commit the baseline artifact as a test fixture.
findings.finding: The before/after dry-run diff test described in AC bullet 6 — capturing `reconcile --dry-run` stdout before and after the change on a fixed fixture store, asserting byte-identical output, and committing a fixture artifact — is a substantial new test deliverable with no corresponding description anywhere in the 'What' section or any other plan body section.
findings.location: AC bullet 6 vs. 'What' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan to the 'Scope' or a new 'Rollback' section — e.g., 'Revert this PR via `git revert <sha>`; the warn-and-ignore config back-compat means re-adding the flags to rebar.toml is not required on rollback' — so operators have a documented recovery path.
findings.finding: The plan permanently deletes code (`_mirrored_equal`, divergence counting, `baseline_shadow_check`/`baseline_shadow_divergence` diagnostics, and possibly `baseline_shadow.py` entirely) and justifies the deletion as safe, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan. The T4 criterion requires a reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement of safety.
findings.location: 'Scope' section ('Backward-compat verdict: SAFE') and 'What' section (deletion instructions)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under 'Scope': 'If a regression is found post-merge, revert this commit (`git revert <sha>`). Because old config keys are warned-and-ignored, no rebar.toml restore is needed. Baselines already written to bindings.json are forward-compatible with the reverted binary (per ADR-0026).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback note to the plan (e.g., in Scope or a new Rollback section) explicitly stating that the only revert path is `git revert <this-commit>` followed by re-deploy, and confirm that `bindings.json` baseline data written during the always-on window remains safe for the reverted binary (per ADR-0026).
findings.finding: The plan removes both config flags without documenting an explicit rollback path. After this change, reverting to the previous dual-write/consume behavior requires a full code revert (git revert), not a config change. While the flags were already ON in production (so no runtime behavior changes), the plan should explicitly acknowledge that the rollback mechanism is 'git revert of this commit' so operators know there is no config knob to pull in an incident.
findings.location: ## Scope / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph 'Rollback' section: 'If this change must be reverted, `git revert <sha>` and re-deploy restores the flag. Baseline data written to `bindings.json` during the hardcoded window is forward- and backward-compatible (ADR-0026) — no data cleanup is required on rollback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify (in plan or code comment) that `binding_store.set_baseline` is a purely in-memory mutation flushed atomically by the subsequent `save()` call, OR if it writes directly to disk, specify how partial-update on crash is detected/recovered.
findings.finding: The plan instructs extracting `set_baseline` advancement into `_advance_baselines` called 'UNCONDITIONALLY before `save()`', but does not clarify whether `binding_store.set_baseline` is an in-memory mutation (flushed atomically by `save()`) or a direct disk write. If it is a direct disk write, a process crash between `_advance_baselines` and `save()` leaves `bindings.json` in a partially-updated state where baselines are advanced but other reconciler state is not persisted, creating an inconsistency window on the next run.
findings.location: What section: 'First EXTRACT the `set_baseline` advancement loop into a standalone helper (e.g. `reconcile._advance_baselines`) called UNCONDITIONALLY before `save()`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence to the 'What' section: '`set_baseline` is an in-memory mutation; `save()` persists the entire binding store atomically (write-then-rename), so no partial-update window exists on crash.' If that is not already true, make `save()` the sole disk writer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a new acceptance criterion specifying a non-dry-run reconcile fixture test that exercises `_advance_baselines` directly: given a fixture `binding_store` with known prior state, run a reconcile pass (or call `_advance_baselines` directly with a fixture snapshot), and assert `binding_store.get_baseline(local_id)` returns the expected post-advance value for each binding — so the always-on write path is pinned independently of dry-run.
findings.finding: The newly hardcoded always-on write path (`_advance_baselines` / `binding_store.set_baseline`) is not covered by any newly-specified behavioral test. AC6 (dry-run fixture test) explicitly uses dry-run, which bypasses all writes to `bindings.json`. AC4 (cold-start unit test) asserts only that a log line fires, not that baselines are correctly advanced. No AC specifies a test that runs a reconcile pass against a fixture store and asserts `get_baseline(local_id)` returns the expected post-advance value — the core correctness property of the new always-on path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (dry-run regression) and AC4 (cold-start log line); What section — `_advance_baselines` extraction
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A new unit/integration test calls `_advance_baselines(curr_snapshot, bindings)` (or runs a non-dry-run reconcile pass on a fixture store) and asserts that `binding_store.get_baseline(local_id)` equals `curr_snapshot[jira_key]` for each confirmed binding after the call — pinning the correctness of the extracted helper independently of the dry-run regression.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `reconcile._advance_baselines` warrants extraction as a named helper (Rule-of-Three: it has 1 call site); if not, inline the 10-line `set_baseline` loop directly into `reconcile.py` at the hardcoded always-on location instead.
findings.finding: The proposed `reconcile._advance_baselines` helper is a single-call-site abstraction. The plan says to 'EXTRACT the set_baseline advancement loop into a standalone helper (e.g. `reconcile._advance_baselines`) called UNCONDITIONALLY before `save()`', but grep confirms this helper does not yet exist and has only one proposed call site. Inlining the loop directly into `reconcile.py` at the same location achieves the same result with less indirection and no new named abstraction.
findings.location: What section: 'First EXTRACT the set_baseline advancement loop into a standalone helper (e.g. reconcile._advance_baselines) called UNCONDITIONALLY before save()'
findings.suggested_fix: Inline the advancement loop directly in `reconcile.py` where the `if _dual_write:` block currently lives, removing the named helper entirely. The loop is short enough to be self-documenting with a one-line comment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the 'What' section the exact emission site for the `RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<id>` log line (module, function, and guard condition), matching how `RECON: baseline_shadow_check` is co-located with its triggering logic in `baseline_shadow.py:105-109`.
findings.finding: The `RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<id>` log line is referenced in AC#4 and the 'Cold-start note' section as a new observable output, but the plan's 'What' task decomposition never specifies the emission site (which function, which module, under what call guard). An implementer must guess where to add it — `_advance_baselines`, `outbound_fields.py:418-419`, or elsewhere. The analogous existing `RECON: baseline_shadow_check` line is co-located in `baseline_shadow.py:105-109` alongside its decision logic, but the plan provides no such co-location guidance for the new cold-start line.
findings.location: Cold-start note section; AC#4; 'What' section (absent)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'In `_advance_baselines` (the extracted helper in `reconcile.py`), after calling `binding_store.get_baseline(local_id)` and before `set_baseline`, emit `RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<local_id>` to stderr when `get_baseline()==None` for a confirmed binding whose jira_key is in the current window. Mirror the flush=True pattern from `baseline_shadow.py:105-109`.'

findings.finding: AC6's proving command includes an escape hatch ('or the equivalent existing reconcile dry-run test staying green') that allows the criterion to be satisfied by pre-existing green tests without ever performing the before/after diff — this is a fixture-invalidity defect (taxonomy #4): a test already green before the change proves nothing about the diff being zero.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (dry-run diff)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC6 text with: 'Capture `reconcile --dry-run` stdout on fixture `tests/fixtures/store_golden.json` before the change (commit the output as `tests/fixtures/dry_run_before.txt`); re-run after the change and assert `diff tests/fixtures/dry_run_before.txt <(reconcile --dry-run)` exits 0. Attach both outputs to the PR. This artifact IS the proof; passing pre-existing tests alone does not satisfy this criterion.'

findings.finding: The plan is ambiguous about what 'remove baseline_shadow.py if fully dead' means in practice: the module performs TWO distinct functions — (1) advancing per-binding baselines from curr_snapshot via set_baseline (the baseline advancement mechanism, still needed after hardcoding always-on), and (2) comparing stored baselines to prev_snapshot for the equivalence check (the rollout gate, now permanently satisfied). If an implementer reads 'fully dead' as 'both flags removed → module unreachable → delete it', they also delete the only per-pass caller of set_baseline, breaking baseline advancement. The plan does not specify where the baseline-advancement responsibility should land if baseline_shadow.py is removed.
findings.location: What section ('remove the now-unreachable shadow/equivalence machinery (baseline_shadow.py) if fully dead') / baseline_shadow.py:41-109 / reconcile.py:938-954
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'When removing baseline_shadow.py, first extract the set_baseline advancement loop (baseline_shadow.py:62-84) into a standalone helper (e.g., reconcile._advance_baselines) called unconditionally before save(); delete only the equivalence-check portions (_mirrored_equal, divergence counting, RECON stderr line). The advancement is not dead — only the divergence gate is.'

findings.finding: AC3 ('Dead shadow machinery removed if fully unreachable, or a comment records why it stays') has no concrete proving command — there is no stated command to verify that `baseline_shadow.py` is absent from the repo or that its public API has no remaining callers.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 (baseline_shadow.py removal)

findings.finding: The cold-start log emission site specified in AC #4 ('the reconciler emits a RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<id> line whenever get_baseline()==None for a confirmed binding') requires binding confirmation state that is not available at the natural detection point (_diff_fields in outbound_fields.py). _diff_fields is reached for both confirmed and pending bindings (pending bindings also have no baseline and also return None from get_baseline), yet the AC qualifies the log to 'confirmed' bindings only. The plan does not specify where the log should be emitted to satisfy both the None-detection and the confirmed-binding qualifier simultaneously.
findings.location: Cold-start note section / AC #4 / outbound_fields.py:417-419 vs outbound_differ.py:712+
findings.suggested_fix: Emit the cold-start log in `_compute_outbound_update_mutation` (outbound_differ.py) immediately after the `get_baseline(local_id)` call returns `None` for a bound (update-path) ticket, rather than inside `_diff_fields`. The update path is only reached when `jira_key is not None` (line 607 of outbound_differ.py), which coincides with confirmed/pending — add a `binding_store.get_state(local_id) == 'confirmed'` guard or document that the update path is close enough to confirmed for this diagnostic.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
